meaning: n.敲打,轻叩,破布vt.叩,敲一下
examples: I knew his opinion carried a lot of clout with them.
Mr. Sutherland may have the clout needed to push the two trading giants into a deal.
Never washed so much as a dish-clout in your life, I'll lay!
But selling is still the hardest part of this business, especially since local suppliers have so much clout.
The agreement gives the soldiers some clout, which is almost certainly to Golkar's advantage.
